summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the dynamic OAuth client-registration flow issues server-side localhost discovery and registration requests when a low-privilege user triggers OAuth auth setup for a crafted generic OAuth2 credential.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show the vulnerable OAuth flow making GET requests to localhost well-known discovery endpoints and a POST request to a localhost registration endpoint, then returning an authorization URI rooted at that attacker-controlled localhost server.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the pinned n8n@2.18.0 source revision, built the reproduction image, verified the mounted checkout commit, and emitted EVOHUNT_PROOF oauth2_localhost_ssrf together with the observed request sequence GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp, GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/issuer, POST /register, plus a localhost authorization URI.
verification_method: The host run script clones and pins the vulnerable repository revision, the container verifies that /target-repo is exactly commit 6336f0a447eab3600b1fe13216f1abd5b6e25dff, and the proof helper asserts the exact localhost request sequence and authorization URI before emitting the unique proof string.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run log contains EVOHUNT_PROOF oauth2_localhost_ssrf.
The proof output records GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp, GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/issuer, and POST /register against a 127.0.0.1 listener.
The proof output includes an authorization URI beginning with http://127.0.0.1:<port>/authorize?.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, verifies the exact commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
